description_long: This is a rectangular, freestanding shelving unit with a white frame and eight cubby compartments arranged in two columns and four rows. Each compartment contains a fabric storage bin, with the top two rows featuring blue bins, the next row green bins, and the bottom row a mix of green and yellow bins. The frame appears to be made of wood or engineered wood, while the bins are likely made of fabric with reinforced edges for structure. The sides and back of the unit are solid and unadorned, emphasizing its utilitarian design. The open-front cubbies allow for easy access to the bins, making it suitable for organizing items in urban environments such as garages, community centers, or public spaces. The overall appearance is modern and functional, with a focus on color-coded organization.
description: A white rectangular shelving unit with eight colorful fabric bins (blue, green, yellow) arranged in cubbies, designed for organized storage.
description_view_0: Front view showing all eight cubby compartments with blue, green, and yellow fabric bins clearly visible.
description_view_1: Side view displaying the solid, plain white side panel of the shelving unit with no visible compartments or bins.
description_view_2: Rear view similar to the front, showing the cubby compartments and bins, but with slight perspective difference.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again showing a plain white side panel with no visible compartments or bins.
